name: Outline API Vocabulary
description: Terms and definitions extracted from the Outline API OpenAPI specification

terms.definition:
`AccessRequests` represent a request by a user for access to a document
they do not currently have permission to view. The request can be approved
or dismissed by a user with permission to share the document.

`Attachments` represent a file uploaded to cloud storage. They are created
before the upload happens from the client and store all the meta information
such as file type, size, and location.
A Auth object in the Outline API

`Collections` represent grouping of documents in the knowledge base, they
offer a way to structure information in a nested hierarchy and a level
at which read and write permissions can be granted to individual users or
groups of users.

`Comments` represent a comment either on a selection of text in a document
or on the document itself.

`DataAttributes` represent custom metadata fields that can be attached to
documents. They allow workspaces to add structured data like status, priority,
or any other custom properties to their documents.

`Documents` are what everything else revolves around. A document represents
a single page of information and always returns the latest version of the
content. Documents are stored in [Markdown](https://spec.commonmark.org/)
formatting.

`Events` represent an artifact of an action. Whether it is creating a user,
editing a document, changing permissions, or any other action – an event
is created that can be used as an audit trail or activity stream.

`FileOperations` represent background jobs for importing or exporting files.
You can query the file operation to find the state of progress and any
resulting output.

`Groups` represent a list of users that logically belong together, for
example there might be groups for each department in your organization.
Groups can be granted access to collections with read or write permissions.

`OAuthClients` represent OAuth clients that can be used to authenticate
users with third-party services.

`OAuthAuthentications` represent individual scoped authentications between
Outline and an `OAuthClient`.

`Revisions` represent a snapshot of a document at a point in time. They
are used to keep track of editing and collaboration history – a document
can also be restored to a previous revision if necessary.

`Shares` represent authorization to view a document without being a member
of the workspace. Shares are created in order to give access to documents publicly.
Each user that shares a document will have a unique share object.

`Stars` represent a favorited document or collection in the application sidebar.
Each user has their own collection of starred items.

`Users` represent an individual with access to the knowledge base. Users
can be created automatically when signing in with SSO or when a user is
invited via email.

`Templates` represent reusable document templates that can be used as a
starting point when creating new documents. Templates can be scoped to a
specific collection or available workspace-wide.

`Views` represent a compressed record of an individual users views of a
document. Individual views are not recorded but a first, last and total
is kept per user.
A Permission object in the Outline API
The editing mode for text updates to a document. When set to `patch`, the `findText` parameter is required and the existing occurrence of `findText` will be replaced with the value of `text`.
A AccessRequest object in the Outline API
Unique identifier for the object.
Identifier for the document this request is for.
Identifier for the user that made the request.
Identifier for the workspace the request belongs to.
The current status of the access request.
Identifier for the user that responded to the request, if any.
The date and time the request was responded to, if any.
The date and time that this object was created
The date and time that this object was last changed
A Attachment object in the Outline API
The size of the attachment in bytes. Returned as a string as the value may exceed the safe integer range.
A Pagination object in the Outline API
A Sorting object in the Outline API
A NavigationNode object in the Outline API
A Collection object in the Outline API
A short unique identifier that can be used to identify the collection instead of the UUID.
A description of the collection, may contain markdown formatting
The collection description as rich-text JSON, when available.
The position of the collection in the sidebar
A color representing the collection, this is used to help make collections more identifiable in the UI. It should be in HEX format including the #
A string that represents an icon in the outline-icons package or an emoji
Whether public document sharing is enabled in this collection
Whether commenting is enabled in this collection
The date and time that this object was deleted
The date and time that this object was archived
Metadata about the external source this collection was imported from, if any.
A Comment object in the Outline API
Identifier for the comment this is a child of, if any.
Identifier for the user who created this comment.
The date and time that this comment was resolved, if it has been.
Identifier for the user who resolved this comment, if any.
List of emoji reactions on this comment.
The document text that the comment is anchored to, only included if includeAnchorText=true.
A DataAttribute object in the Outline API
Whether this data attribute is pinned to the top of documents.
The data type of the attribute value.
Additional options for certain data attribute types.
A DocumentDataAttribute object in the Outline API
Unique identifier for the associated data attribute.
The value of the data attribute for this document.
A Document object in the Outline API
Identifier for the associated collection.
Identifier for the document this is a child of, if any.
Whether this document should be displayed in a full-width view.
The text content of the document, contains markdown formatting
Identifiers of users who have edited the document.
Task completion counts for the document.
Unique identifier for the template this document was created from, if any
A number that is auto incrementing with every revision of the document that is saved
The date and time that this object was published
A rollup of activity counts for a document over a daily or weekly period.
The UTC day the rollup represents. For weekly rollups this is the first day (Monday) of the week.
The length of time the rollup covers. Daily rollups are stored for recent activity, older rollups are aggregated into weekly buckets.
Total number of document views on this day.
Number of unique viewers on this day.
Total comments made on this day.
Total reactions added on this day.
Number of document revisions on this day.
Number of unique editors on this day.
A Event object in the Outline API
Identifier for the object this event is associated with when it is not one of document, collection, or user.
The user that performed the action.
The ip address the action was performed from. This field is only returned when the `auditLog` boolean is true.
The set of changes made by this event. This field is only returned when the `auditLog` boolean is true.
A Error object in the Outline API
A FileOperation object in the Outline API
The type of file operation.
The file format of the resulting file.
The state of the file operation.
A Group object in the Outline API
An identifier for this group in an external system, if linked.
Whether mentioning this group is disabled.
Details of the linked external group, if any.
The number of users that are members of the group
A OAuthClient object in the Outline API
The name of the developer who created this OAuth client.
The URL of the developer who created this OAuth client.
A URL pointing to an image representing the OAuth client.
The client ID for the OAuth client.
The client secret for the OAuth client.
The type of the OAuth client.
The redirect URIs for the OAuth client.
Whether the OAuth client is available to other workspaces.
Date and time when this OAuth client was last used.
A OAuthAuthentication object in the Outline API
Identifier for the associated OAuthClient.
A reduced, public representation of the associated OAuth client.
A Revision object in the Outline API
A Share object in the Outline API
Title of the shared document.
URL of the original document.
Title of the shared document or collection.
Path of the shared document or collection.
Custom domain the share is served on, if any.
URL of an icon displayed on the publicly shared page, overriding the workspace branding.
If to also give permission to view documents nested beneath this one.
Whether visitors to the public share can subscribe to receive email notifications when the document is updated. Requires SMTP to be configured on the workspace.
Whether the shared page may be indexed by search engines.
Whether to show the last-updated time on the shared page.
Whether to show a table of contents on the shared page.
The number of times the shared page has been viewed.
Date and time when this share was last viewed. Only returned to workspace admins.
A Star object in the Outline API
A Team object in the Outline API
If set then the referenced collection is where users will be redirected to after signing in instead of the Home screen
Whether members are allowed to create new collections. If false then only admins can create collections.
Whether members are allowed to create new groups. If false then only admins can create groups.
Whether this workspace has embeds in documents globally enabled. It can be disabled to reduce potential data leakage to third parties.
Whether an invite is required to join this workspace, if false users may join with a linked SSO provider.
Whether this workspace has guest signin enabled. Guests can signin with an email address and are not required to have a Google Workspace/Slack SSO account once invited.
Represents the subdomain at which this workspace's knowledge base can be accessed.
Whether passkey authentication is enabled for this workspace.
Workspace-level preference flags.
Guidance text provided to MCP integrations.
A User object in the Outline API
The email associated with this user, it is migrated from Slack or Google Workspace when the SSO connection is made but can be changed if necessary.
Whether this user has been suspended.
The timezone this user has registered.
A Invite object in the Outline API
A UserRole object in the Outline API
A CollectionStatus object in the Outline API
A Membership object in the Outline API
Identifier for the membership this one was inherited from, if any.
A SearchResult object in the Outline API
The user-provided search query
An answer to the query, if possible
The source of the query
A Policy object in the Outline API
The abilities that are allowed by this policy, if an array is returned then the individual ID's in the array represent the memberships that grant the ability.
A single permission granted by a policy
A GroupMembership object in the Outline API
Identifier for the associated group.
A CollectionGroupMembership object in the Outline API
A Template object in the Outline API
A View object in the Outline API
When the document was first viewed by the user
When the document was last viewed by the user
The number of times the user has viewed the document.
